summary: Frontend testing for Laravel 12 'Smart Inventory AI' dashboard + branded auth. Ran comprehensive Playwright tests covering root redirect, login/register/forgot-password branded pages, empty/invalid credential validation, admin+manager+staff logins, dashboard components (sidebar, topbar, 6 stat cards with expected values, monthly chart, AI recommendation card with 4 items, 5 quick actions, recent-in/recent-out/low-stock lists), sidebar disabled 'soon' modules, logout, profile page, and mobile responsive hamburger. 53/54 assertions passed.
action_items: MINOR (optional): Login empty-form validation - inputs have HTML5 `required` attr so browser blocks submission before Laravel server-side rose-colored errors can render. If the spec explicitly requires 'rose colored validation text' visible on empty submit, remove `required` attribute on email/password inputs (or use novalidate on form) so Laravel validation errors render server-side. Current behavior is valid UX but doesn't match literal spec wording.
critical_code_review_comments: login.blade.php inputs use HTML5 `required` which shortcuts Laravel validation for empty submits - inconsistent with server-side @error rose display shown for other invalid states.
Overall Blade component composition is clean (stat-card, ai-card, quick-action, section-card reusable components used correctly).
test_credentials: admin@demo.com / password (Admin) - VERIFIED; manager@demo.com / password (Manager) - VERIFIED; staff@demo.com / password (Staff) - VERIFIED
seed_data_creation: None created. DemoDataSeeder values verified: 12 products, 4 suppliers, 2 low-stock, 2 out-of-stock, in-today=8, out-today=5, monthly chart 6 months (Feb-Jul 2026), AI recommendation shows 4 rule-based cards.
context_for_next_testing_agent: First iteration - all core dashboard + auth flows verified end-to-end on preview URL. Only nit is empty-form validation on /login uses HTML5 required (see action_items). CRUD modules for Master Barang/Kategori/Supplier/etc are intentionally 'soon' badge disabled in sidebar and out of scope for Task 1. When testing next task iteration, note the top-bar has data-testid=topbar-toggle-sidebar-btn (visible only <lg viewport) and app-sidebar drawer uses Alpine.js.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no bugs found. The one non-passing check (empty form validation) is expected behavior given HTML5 `required` attributes intercept before Laravel server-side validation runs.
